economic_notes: Advance of $3,400,000 to B.I.L. Cree Nation Corporation (owned 51% by Pee-Wey Holdings Inc), which earns royalty and investment income. COVID-19 impacts noted as subsequent event. New First Nations Finance Authority promissory note of $9,443,588 obtained during year.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2021 but audited October 16, 2024 — a very late audit. Consolidated entities include Big Island Lake Cree Nation, Big Island Lake CMHC Housing Program, and Pee-Wey Holdings Inc. B.I.L. Cree Nation Corporation (51% owned by Pee-Wey Holdings) is excluded from consolidation as a business entity. ISC funding reconciliation shows total ISC funding per confirmation of $22,426,751 but recognized revenue of $21,061,209 due to deferred capital project revenue. Restricted cash includes $4,262,577 short-term (ISC capital projects) and $778,413 long-term (CMHC reserves and Debt Reserve Fund). Large new FNFA promissory note of $9,443,588 issued during year. CMHC replacement and operating reserves are underfunded. No schedule of remuneration included in provided text. Loss on disposal of tangible capital assets of $269,448 recorded as other expense. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($21,061,209) + CMHC ($294,227) + FSIN ($319,904) + BATC ($45,500) + TSEC ($35,396) = $21,756,236; osr = $24,790,800 - $21,756,236 = $3,034,564. Note: osr and total_government_transfers adjusted based on identifiable government sources.
warnings: High ISC dependency (84.96%) — operations heavily reliant on federal transfers
